title: × agrositanion saxicola
intro: × Agrositanion saxicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the St. Anthony's herb. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of × Agrositanion saxicola include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the healing process of minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions or tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
